structured.name: Start Transaction - Id not Accepted
structured.objective:
To enable the Charging Station to suspend a transaction when the IdToken has an
AuthorizationStatus that does not allow charging.
structured.description:
This use case covers how the Charging Station wants to start a transaction while the IdToken is
not accepted by the CSMS.
Since the identifier might have been authorized locally by the Charging Station using outdated
information, the CSMS has to validate the IdTokenType in every TransactionEventRequest
message it receives that contains an idToken. When receiving a TransactionEventResponse
message with idTokenInfo.status is not Accepted, the Charging Station will stop the energy
delivery to the EV (depending on MaxEnergyOnInvalidId).
scenario:
1. The Charging Station sends TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Started) that contains the
IdToken provided by the EV Driver.

2. The CSMS responds with TransactionEventResponse, with an idTokenInfo.status that does not
allow charging.

3. The Charging Station suspends the energy offer. (Taking into account:
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId, if supported)

4. If StopTxOnInvalidId = false, then
4a. The Charging Station sends TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Updated) with
triggerReason = ChargingStateChanged and the chargingState = SuspendedEVSE and
receives TransactionEventResponse from the CSMS.

5. If StopTxOnInvalidId = true, then
5a. If TxStopPoint = "EVConnected" the Charging Station sends TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Updated) with triggerReason = Deauthorized and the chargingState =
EVConnected and receives TransactionEventResponse from the CSMS.
else
5b. If TxStopPoint = "Authorized" the Charging Station sends TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Ended) with triggerReason = Deauthorized and the chargingState =
EVConnected and receives TransactionEventResponse from the CSMS.
structured.prerequisites:
The EV Driver is offline/locally authorized by the Charging Station.
The IdToken is not allowed to charge by the CSMS.
structured.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The transaction is kept ongoing, and the cable remains locked, but no energy is delivered.
Failure postcondition:
n/a
structured.remarks:
The scenario description and sequence diagram above are based on the Configuration Variable
for start & stop transaction being configured as follows:
TxStartPoint: PowerPathClosed
TxStopPoint: either EVConnected or Authorized/PowerPathClosed
This use-case is also valid for other configurations, but then the transaction might start/stop at
another moment, which might change the sequence in which message are sent. For more details
see the use cases: E01 - Start Transaction options and E06 - Stop Transaction options.
NOTE: The configuration parameter MaxEnergyOnInvalidId only plays a role when
StopTxOnInvalidId is set to false.

requirements.definition:
The CSMS MUST verify validity of the identifier in
the TransactionEventRequest message.

requirements.precondition:
E05.FR.01 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is set to false
AND
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId is not
implemented or has been exceeded.
TxStopPoint does NOT contain:
EnergyTransfer
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL stop the energy
delivery to the EV immediately and send
TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Updated)
with triggerReason set to ChargingStateChanged
and chargingState set to SuspendedEVSE

requirements.precondition:
E05.FR.01 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is set to false
AND
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId is set and
has NOT been exceeded.
requirements.definition:
Energy delivery to the EV SHALL be allowed until
the amount of energy specified in
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId has been reached.

requirements.precondition:
When a TransactionEventRequest has
to be created
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL set the message’s
seqNo field as specified in Sequence Number
Generation.

requirements.precondition:
When configured to send meter data
in the TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Started), See: Meter
Values - Configuration AND
EVSE is known at start of transaction
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL add the configured
measurands to the optional meterValue field with
context = Transaction.Begin in the
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Started)
sent to the CSMS to provide more details during the
transaction.

The Charging Station SHALL retrieve signed meter
values and put them in the signedMeterValue field
of sampledValues.

requirements.precondition:
When configured to send meter data
in the TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Started), See: Meter
Values - Configuration AND
EVSE is not known at start of
transaction
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL add the measurands
for eventType = Started to the optional
meterValue field with context =
Transaction.Begin in the
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Updated)
that occurs when charging starts.

requirements.precondition:
E05.FR.01 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is true AND
TxStopPoint does NOT contain:
(Authorized OR PowerPathClosed OR
EnergyTransfer)
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL stop the energy
transfer and send TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Updated) with triggerReason set to
Deauthorized and in the same or next
TransactionEventRequest report chargingState set
preferably to EVConnected, or alternatively to
SuspendedEVSE.

requirements.precondition:
E05.FR.01 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is true AND
TxStopPoint does contain:
(Authorized OR PowerPathClosed OR
EnergyTransfer)
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHALL stop the transaction
and send TransactionEventRequest (eventType =
Ended) with triggerReason set to Deauthorized and
stoppedReason set to DeAuthorized.

requirements.precondition:
E05.FR.10 AND
If the Charging Station has the
possibility to lock the Charging Cable
requirements.definition:
The Charging Station SHOULD keep the Charging
Cable locked until the owner presents his identifier.

sub_scenarios.objective:
Stop a transaction when communication between the Charging Station and the EV is lost. (for
example: cable unplugged)
steps: 1. Communication between Charging Station and the EV is lost (Charging cable is unplugged).

2. If charging cable unplugged on the Charging Station side: send StatusNotificationRequest to
the CSMS to inform it about a Connector that became Available.

3. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended) notifying the
CSMS about a transaction that has ended.

4. The CSMS responds with a TransactionEventResponse, confirming that the
TransactionEventRequest was received.
sub_scenarios.prerequisites:
A transaction is ongoing.
Configuration Variable: TxStopPoint contains: EVConnected

sub_scenarios.objective: Stop a transaction when the driver is no longer authorized.
steps: 1. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest to the CSMS.
2. An invalid IdToken is received in a TransactionEventResponse.

3. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended) notifying the
CSMS about a transaction that has ended.

4. The CSMS responds with a TransactionEventResponse, confirming that the
TransactionEventRequest was received.
sub_scenarios.prerequisites:
A transaction is ongoing.
Configuration Variable: TxStopPoint contains: Authorized
sub_scenarios.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The transaction is ended and the CSMS is Successfully informed.
Failure postcondition:
The transaction is still ongoing. or
The CSMS is not informed.

sub_scenarios.objective: Stop a transaction when the EV driver is no longer authorized and/or the EV is disconnected.
steps: 1. The Charging Station is disconnected from EV and/or the EV driver is no longer authorized.

2. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended) notifying the
CSMS about a transaction that has ended.

3. The CSMS responds with a TransactionEventResponse, confirming that the
TransactionEventRequest was received.
sub_scenarios.prerequisites:
A transaction is ongoing.
Configuration Variable: TxStopPoint contains: PowerPathClosed
sub_scenarios.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The transaction is ended and the CSMS is Successfully informed.
Failure postcondition:
The transaction is still ongoing. or
The CSMS is not informed.

sub_scenarios.objective:
Stop a transaction when energy transfer stops. This will also mean the transaction stops when
the EV stops taking energy, for example when the battery is too hot.
steps:
1. The energy transfer between EV and the Charging Station stops (for example: EV stops
charging).

2. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended) notifying the
CSMS about a transaction that has ended.

3. The CSMS responds with a TransactionEventResponse, confirming that the
TransactionEventRequest was received.
sub_scenarios.prerequisites:
A transaction is ongoing.
Configuration Variable: TxStopPoint contains: EnergyTransfer
sub_scenarios.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The transaction is ended and the CSMS is Successfully informed.
Failure postcondition:
The transaction is still ongoing. or
The CSMS is not informed.

sub_scenarios.objective: Stop a transaction when EV driver ends authorization
steps: 1. The EV drivers presents an IdToken to end the charging.

2. The Charging Station sends a TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Ended) notifying the
CSMS about a transaction that has ended.

3. The CSMS responds with a TransactionEventResponse, confirming that the
TransactionEventRequest was received.
sub_scenarios.prerequisites:
A transaction is ongoing.
Configuration Variable: TxStopPoint contains: Authorized (or PowerPathClosed).
sub_scenarios.postconditions:
Successful postcondition:
The transaction is ended and the CSMS is Successfully informed.
Failure postcondition:
The transaction is still ongoing. or
The CSMS is not informed.

chunks.content:
# E05 - Start Transaction - Id not Accepted

**Objective:** To enable the Charging Station to suspend a transaction when the IdToken has an
AuthorizationStatus that does not allow charging.

**Description:** This use case covers how the Charging Station wants to start a transaction while the IdToken is
not accepted by the CSMS.
Since the identifier might have been authorized locally by the Charging Station using outdated
information, the CSMS has to validate the IdTokenType in every TransactionEventRequest
message it receives that contains an idToken. When receiving a TransactionEventResponse
message with idTokenInfo.status is not Accepted, the Charging Station will stop the energy
delivery to the EV (depending on MaxEnergyOnInvalidId).

**Actors:** Charging Station, CSMS

**Scenario:**
1. The Charging Station sends TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Started) that contains the
IdToken provided by the EV Driver.
2. The CSMS responds with TransactionEventResponse, with an idTokenInfo.status that does not
allow charging.
3. The Charging Station suspends the energy offer. (Taking into account:
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId, if supported)
4. If StopTxOnInvalidId = false, then
4a. The Charging Station sends TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Updated) with
triggerReason = ChargingStateChanged and the chargingState = SuspendedEVSE and
receives TransactionEventResponse from the CSMS.
5. If StopTxOnInvalidId = true, then
5a. If TxStopPoint = "EVConnected" the Charging Station sends TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Updated) with triggerReason = Deauthorized and the chargingState =
EVConnected and receives TransactionEventResponse from the CSMS.
else
5b. If TxStopPoint = "Authorized" the Charging Station sends TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Ended) with triggerReason = Deauthorized and the chargingState =
EVConnected and receives TransactionEventResponse from the CSMS.

**Prerequisites:** The EV Driver is offline/locally authorized by the Charging Station.
The IdToken is not allowed to charge by the CSMS.

**Postconditions:** Successful postcondition:
The transaction is kept ongoing, and the cable remains locked, but no energy is delivered.
Failure postcondition:
n/a

**Remarks:** The scenario description and sequence diagram above are based on the Configuration Variable
for start & stop transaction being configured as follows:
TxStartPoint: PowerPathClosed
TxStopPoint: either EVConnected or Authorized/PowerPathClosed
This use-case is also valid for other configurations, but then the transaction might start/stop at
another moment, which might change the sequence in which message are sent. For more details
see the use cases: E01 - Start Transaction options and E06 - Stop Transaction options.
NOTE: The configuration parameter MaxEnergyOnInvalidId only plays a role when
StopTxOnInvalidId is set to false.

metadata.heading: E05 - Start Transaction - Id not Accepted

chunks.content:
# E05 - Requirements

**E05.FR.01**
The CSMS MUST verify validity of the identifier in
the TransactionEventRequest message.

**E05.FR.02**
Precondition: E05.FR.01 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is set to false
AND
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId is not
implemented or has been exceeded.
TxStopPoint does NOT contain:
EnergyTransfer
The Charging Station SHALL stop the energy
delivery to the EV immediately and send
TransactionEventRequest (eventType = Updated)
with triggerReason set to ChargingStateChanged
and chargingState set to SuspendedEVSE

**E05.FR.03**
Precondition: E05.FR.01 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is set to false
AND
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId is set and
has NOT been exceeded.
Energy delivery to the EV SHALL be allowed until
the amount of energy specified in
MaxEnergyOnInvalidId has been reached.

**E05.FR.04**
Precondition: When a TransactionEventRequest has
to be created
The Charging Station SHALL set the message’s
seqNo field as specified in Sequence Number
Generation.

**E05.FR.05**
Precondition: When configured to send meter data
in the TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Started), See: Meter
Values - Configuration AND
EVSE is known at start of transaction
The Charging Station SHALL add the configured
measurands to the optional meterValue field with
context = Transaction.Begin in the
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Started)
sent to the CSMS to provide more details during the
transaction.

**E05.FR.06**
Precondition: SampledDataSignReadings is true
The Charging Station SHALL retrieve signed meter
values and put them in the signedMeterValue field
of sampledValues.

**E05.FR.08**
Precondition: When configured to send meter data
in the TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Started), See: Meter
Values - Configuration AND
EVSE is not known at start of
transaction
The Charging Station SHALL add the measurands
for eventType = Started to the optional
meterValue field with context =
Transaction.Begin in the
TransactionEventRequest(eventType = Updated)
that occurs when charging starts.

# E05 - Requirements (continued)

**E05.FR.09**
Precondition: E05.FR.01 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is true AND
TxStopPoint does NOT contain:
(Authorized OR PowerPathClosed OR
EnergyTransfer)
The Charging Station SHALL stop the energy
transfer and send TransactionEventRequest
(eventType = Updated) with triggerReason set to
Deauthorized and in the same or next
TransactionEventRequest report chargingState set
preferably to EVConnected, or alternatively to
SuspendedEVSE.

**E05.FR.10**
Precondition: E05.FR.01 AND
The authorization status in
TransactionEventResponse is not
Accepted AND
The transaction is still ongoing AND
StopTxOnInvalidId is true AND
TxStopPoint does contain:
(Authorized OR PowerPathClosed OR
EnergyTransfer)
The Charging Station SHALL stop the transaction
and send TransactionEventRequest (eventType =
Ended) with triggerReason set to Deauthorized and
stoppedReason set to DeAuthorized.

**E05.FR.11**
Precondition: E05.FR.10 AND
If the Charging Station has the
possibility to lock the Charging Cable
The Charging Station SHOULD keep the Charging
Cable locked until the owner presents his identifier.
